author: Ameen Hassan
summary: The session focused on cleaning up a GitHub repository called data-qualification-agent (under ahassan507) that the user had pushed and did not want others to see. The assistant had already used git rm --cached to untrack files in the reports/ directory from git, but the files still existed on disk. The user expressed concern about the repo being visible or traceable. The assistant proposed replacing the repo with a fresh one with no git history. The user agreed. The assistant attempted to delete the existing GitHub repo but failed with HTTP 403 due to missing delete_repo scope. The user was instructed to run gh auth refresh to add that scope and confirmed completion. The assistant then successfully deleted the repo, re-initialized git locally with a fresh history, and began staging files while noticing .claude/settings.local.json was being staged and flagging it for exclusion.
problems: The data-qualification-agent repo had been pushed to GitHub with history that the user did not want exposed
Report files in reports/ had been tracked by git and needed to be removed from tracking
GitHub CLI lacked the delete_repo scope, blocking repo deletion
approaches: Used git rm --cached to untrack report files from git without deleting them from disk
Deleted the existing GitHub repo and recreated it with a fresh local git history (no prior commits)
Ran gh auth refresh to add delete_repo scope to GitHub CLI
results: Report files (360chi_health_check_report.docx/html/json/md) confirmed still on disk after git rm --cached
Local git history stripped and re-initialized with git init
Files re-staged for a clean initial commit
.claude/settings.local.json flagged as a sensitive file that should be excluded from the new commit

decisions.decision: Replace existing repo with a fresh repo that has no prior git history
Keep the repo private going forward

open_questions: Was the fresh repo successfully pushed to GitHub after the history strip?
Was .claude/settings.local.json properly excluded before the clean commit was made?
Are there other sensitive files in the staged set that should be excluded?
